text: High credit utilization (e.g., >70%) is associated with higher default risk because it indicates limited remaining credit capacity.
Short credit history increases uncertainty in repayment behavior and can increase estimated risk when other factors are unfavorable.
High debt-to-income ratio reduces repayment capacity and is a common driver of credit risk.
Recent delinquency or late payments are strong indicators of elevated default risk.
Case: Applicant rejected. Utilization 82%, credit history 1.1 years, DTI 41%. Main reasons: high utilization and short history.
Case: Applicant rejected. Utilization 76%, recent late payment, DTI 38%. Main reasons: delinquency signal and high utilization.
Case: Applicant approved with conditions. Utilization 35%, credit history 6.0 years, stable income. Main reasons: low utilization and long history.
Case: Applicant rejected. Very short history 0.8 years and high DTI 45% despite moderate utilization 55%. Main reasons: short history and high DTI.
Case: Applicant approved. Utilization 28%, no delinquency, DTI 22%. Main reasons: strong repayment capacity and low utilization.
Case: Applicant rejected. Multiple recent late payments and utilization 69%. Main reasons: delinquency and elevated utilization.
Case: Applicant approved. Utilization 40%, credit history 8.2 years, DTI 27%. Main reasons: long history and manageable debt burden.
Case: Applicant rejected. Utilization 88%, credit history 2.0 years, DTI 36%. Main reasons: extremely high utilization.
